contest.title: Neowise Labs Contest 1 (Codeforces Round 1018, Div. 1 + Div. 2)

sources.notes: Statement and constraints: recover one possible initially on bulb after repeatedly toggling a fixed four-cell shape on an infinite integer grid.
Official tutorial confirms the parity invariants on vertical lines x=c and diagonals x+y=c, whose odd-count intersection gives the treasure.
Luogu solution provides an additional invariant-based explanation for the toggling operation.

annotation.summary: Given the final set of on bulbs after toggling a fixed four-cell pattern any number of times from one initial on bulb, output any possible initial coordinate.
annotation.constraints: Sum n <= 200000 and coordinates are large; the solution must be linear or near-linear in the number of on bulbs.
annotation.core_idea: Every operation toggles two bulbs on vertical line x=u and two bulbs on vertical line x=u+1, so the parity of the number of on bulbs on each vertical line is invariant except for the initial bulb's x-line. Thus exactly one x-coordinate has odd count, and it is s. The same operation toggles two bulbs on diagonal x+y=u+v and two bulbs on diagonal x+y=u+v+1, so exactly one x+y value has odd count, and it is s+t. Count final bulbs by x and by x+y, find the odd keys, and output (s, (s+t)-s).
annotation.complexity: O(n log n) with maps or O(n) expected with hash maps
tricks: Use parity invariants over families of lines.
Group final points by x-coordinate and by x+y diagonal.
Recover the point as the intersection of the odd vertical line and odd diagonal.

solution_variants.name: odd-line-intersection
solution_variants.summary: Maintain parity counts for every x and every x+y among lit bulbs. The guaranteed valid input ensures a unique odd x-line and odd diagonal; their intersection is a valid original bulb.
solution_variants.complexity: O(n log n) or O(n) expected

tags.evidence: The solution depends on odd/even counts of lit bulbs on vertical lines and diagonals.
Each toggle operation preserves parity on every vertical-line family and every x+y diagonal family except for the original single bulb contribution.
Counting bulbs by x-coordinate identifies the unique vertical line containing the initial bulb.
Counting bulbs by x+y diagonal identifies the diagonal containing the initial bulb.
The output is constructed directly as the intersection of two invariant-selected line families.
